question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the BLS on April 10, 2026, showing CPI increased only 0.3% for the month, well below the 0.8% threshold. This makes the probability of the market resolving YES exactly 0%, creating a massive arbitrage opportunity since the market is still priced at 64%.
key_factors: Official BLS data already released showing 0.3% monthly increase
Market resolution criterion of >0.8% was not met
No uncertainty remains as official data is final
risks: Market may not have incorporated the released data yet
Potential for data revisions (though extremely unlikely to change 0.3% to >0.8%)
Settlement/liquidity risks if market closes before resolution
